@@ -238,7 +238,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1z91","title":"Redis Pub/Sub and Streams: real-time messaging with ack semantics","description":"# Redis Pub/Sub and Streams Implementation

## Overview
Extends Redis client with Pub/Sub for real-time messaging and Streams for
durable, consumer-group-based message processing.

## Pub/Sub

### Design
```rust
pub struct RedisPubSub {
    conn: RedisConnection,  // Dedicated connection for subscriptions
    subscriptions: HashSet<String>,
}

impl RedisPubSub {
    /// Create from client (uses dedicated connection).
    pub async fn new(cx: &Cx, client: &RedisClient) -> Outcome<Self, RedisError> {
        let conn = client.get_dedicated(cx).await?;
        Ok(Self { conn, subscriptions: HashSet::new() })
    }
    
    /// Subscribe to channels.
    pub async fn subscribe(&mut self, cx: &Cx, channels: &[&str]) -> Outcome<(), RedisError> {
        for ch in channels {
            self.subscriptions.insert(ch.to_string());
        }
        self.conn.cmd(&["SUBSCRIBE"].iter().chain(channels).collect::<Vec<_>>()).await?;
        Ok(())
    }
    
    /// Subscribe to patterns.
    pub async fn psubscribe(&mut self, cx: &Cx, patterns: &[&str]) -> Outcome<(), RedisError>;
    
    /// Receive next message.
    pub async fn recv(&mut self, cx: &Cx) -> Outcome<PubSubMessage, RedisError> {
        cx.checkpoint()?;
        let resp = self.conn.recv().await?;
        // Parse: ["message", channel, payload]
        Ok(PubSubMessage { channel, payload })
    }
    
    /// Unsubscribe from channels.
    pub async fn unsubscribe(&mut self, cx: &Cx, channels: &[&str]) -> Outcome<(), RedisError>;
}

pub struct PubSubMessage {
    pub channel: String,
    pub payload: Bytes,
}
```

### Publisher
```rust
impl RedisClient {
    /// Publish to channel.
    pub async fn publish(
        &self,
        cx: &Cx,
        channel: &str,
        message: &[u8],
    ) -> Outcome<i64, RedisError> {
        // Returns number of subscribers that received the message
        self.cmd(cx, &["PUBLISH", channel, message]).await?.as_int()
    }
}
```

## Streams

### Design
```rust
pub struct RedisStreams {
    client: RedisClient,
}

impl RedisStreams {
    /// Add entry to stream.
    pub async fn xadd(
        &self,
        cx: &Cx,
        stream: &str,
        id: &str,  // "*" for auto-generate
        fields: &[(&str, &[u8])],
    ) -> Outcome<String, RedisError> {
        let mut args = vec\!["XADD", stream, id];
        for (k, v) in fields {
            args.push(k);
            args.push(std::str::from_utf8(v)?);
        }
        self.client.cmd(cx, &args).await?.as_string()
    }
    
    /// Read from stream.
    pub async fn xread(
        &self,
        cx: &Cx,
        streams: &[(&str, &str)],  // (stream, last_id)
        count: Option<usize>,
        block: Option<Duration>,
    ) -> Outcome<Vec<StreamEntry>, RedisError>;
    
    /// Create consumer group.
    pub async fn xgroup_create(
        &self,
        cx: &Cx,
        stream: &str,
        group: &str,
        start_id: &str,
    ) -> Outcome<(), RedisError>;
    
    /// Read as consumer group member.
    pub async fn xreadgroup(
        &self,
        cx: &Cx,
        group: &str,
        consumer: &str,
        streams: &[(&str, &str)],
        count: Option<usize>,
        block: Option<Duration>,
    ) -> Outcome<Vec<StreamEntry>, RedisError>;
    
    /// Acknowledge message.
    pub async fn xack(
        &self,
        cx: &Cx,
        stream: &str,
        group: &str,
        ids: &[&str],
    ) -> Outcome<i64, RedisError>;
}

pub struct StreamEntry {
    pub id: String,
    pub fields: HashMap<String, Bytes>,
}
```

### Cancel-Correct Consumption
```rust
pub struct StreamConsumer {
    streams: RedisStreams,
    group: String,
    consumer: String,
    pending: Vec<String>,  // IDs to ack
}

impl StreamConsumer {
    /// Get next message. Must be acked or it will be re-delivered.
    pub async fn next(&mut self, cx: &Cx) -> Outcome<Option<StreamMessage>, RedisError> {
        let entries = self.streams.xreadgroup(
            cx, &self.group, &self.consumer, &[/* streams */], Some(1), Some(Duration::from_secs(1))
        ).await?;
        
        if let Some(entry) = entries.first() {
            self.pending.push(entry.id.clone());
            Ok(Some(StreamMessage { entry, consumer: self }))
        } else {
            Ok(None)
        }
    }
}

pub struct StreamMessage<'a> {
    entry: StreamEntry,
    consumer: &'a mut StreamConsumer,
}

impl<'a> StreamMessage<'a> {
    /// Acknowledge processing complete.
    pub async fn ack(self, cx: &Cx) -> Outcome<(), RedisError> {
        self.consumer.streams.xack(
            cx,
            /* stream */,
            &self.consumer.group,
            &[&self.entry.id],
        ).await?;
        self.consumer.pending.retain(|id| id \!= &self.entry.id);
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

## Dependencies
- Requires: Redis client core (bd-9vfn)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pub/Sub subscribe/publish
- [ ] Pattern subscriptions
- [ ] Streams XADD/XREAD
- [ ] Consumer groups
- [ ] Message acknowledgement
- [ ] Unacked messages redeliver
- [ ] Integration tests with Redis","notes":"## Testing Requirements

### Pub/Sub Unit Tests
- `redis::pubsub::subscribe_single_channel` - Subscribe to one channel
- `redis::pubsub::subscribe_multiple_channels` - Subscribe to many channels
- `redis::pubsub::psubscribe_pattern` - Pattern subscriptions
- `redis::pubsub::publish_receive` - Publish and receive message
- `redis::pubsub::unsubscribe` - Unsubscribe from channel
- `redis::pubsub::message_parsing` - Parse pubsub message format

### Streams Unit Tests
- `redis::streams::xadd_auto_id` - Add with auto-generated ID
- `redis::streams::xadd_explicit_id` - Add with explicit ID
- `redis::streams::xread_single` - Read from single stream
- `redis::streams::xread_multiple` - Read from multiple streams
- `redis::streams::xread_block` - Blocking read with timeout
- `redis::streams::xgroup_create` - Create consumer group
- `redis::streams::xreadgroup` - Read as group member
- `redis::streams::xack` - Acknowledge message
- `redis::streams::pending_entries` - Check pending list

### Cancel-Correctness Tests
- `redis::cancel::cancel_subscribe_recv` - Cancel during recv()
- `redis::cancel::cancel_xread_block` - Cancel during blocking XREAD
- `redis::cancel::region_close_pubsub` - Cleanup active subscriptions
- `redis::cancel::unacked_stream_message` - Handle unacked on cancel

### Integration Tests (requires Redis)
- `redis::integration::pubsub_round_trip` - Full pub/sub cycle
- `redis::integration::pubsub_multi_subscriber` - Multiple subscribers
- `redis::integration::streams_consumer_group` - Full consumer group flow
- `redis::integration::streams_redelivery` - Unacked message redelivery
- `redis::integration::streams_claim` - Claim pending messages

### Logging Requirements
- TRACE: Individual commands, message payloads
- DEBUG: Subscribe/unsubscribe, message counts
- INFO: Connection lifecycle, group operations
- WARN: Unacked messages on drop, slow operations
- ERROR: Protocol errors, connection failures

### Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
export RUST_LOG="asupersync::mq::redis=debug,test=info"
export REDIS_URL="redis://localhost:6379"

# Start Redis if needed
docker run -d --name redis-test -p 6379:6379 redis:7 || true

cargo test -p asupersync redis::pubsub:: redis::streams:: -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee redis_pubsub_tests.log

docker stop redis-test || true
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T01:25:41.475801698Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T06:49:48.066679155Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T06:49:48.066534256Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ecosystem-parity","messaging","redis"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1z91","depends_on_id":"bd-2aiq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1z91","depends_on_id":"bd-9vfn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1zaql","title":"Local cancel promotion parity","description":"Make local cancellation promotion match global cancel semantics.\n\nScope:\n- Local cancel waker should inject/promote even if task already scheduled.\n- Align local cancel path with global cancel path (PriorityScheduler::schedule_cancel vs inject).\n- Ensure repeated cancel requests are idempotent.\n\nAcceptance criteria:\n- Cancelling a local task guarantees it is observed promptly even if queued in ready/timed.\n- Behavior matches global cancel semantics for promotion.\n- Add a regression test: local task scheduled in ready/timed then cancelled; verify cancel path executes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrightRiver","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:34:16.377302315Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T20:30:21.177804969Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T20:30:21.177740419Z","close_reason":"Local cancel promotion now matches global semantics. Changed schedule_local_cancel() and ThreeLaneLocalCancelWaker::schedule() to use move_to_cancel_lane() instead of schedule_cancel(), preventing silent cancel drops when tasks are already in ready/timed lanes. 5 regression tests added in priority.rs: ready→cancel promotion, timed→cancel promotion, idempotent priority update, silent-drop demonstration, repeated cancel idempotency. Changes included in df22468.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1zaql","depends_on_id":"bd-1iqbb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:34:16.391054673Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1zho","title":"[ALIEN][RAPTORQ] Spec: RFC-6330-grade scope + determinism + API contract","description":"## Purpose

Lock down what "RFC-6330-grade RaptorQ" means for Asupersync so implementation is coherent, deterministic, and testable.

This spec is intentionally written so we can implement without constantly re-reading external standards; where we intentionally diverge from RFC 6330, we must document it explicitly.

## Core deliverable

Replace the current simplified LT-style XOR construction with a true Raptor-style code:
- systematic transmission (source symbols first)
- robust soliton-style LT layer for repair symbols
- a deterministic precode (LDPC/HDPC-style constraints or equivalent)
- deterministic inactivation decoding (peeling + sparse elimination)
- proof-carrying decode trace artifact (which equations were used)

## Terms / model

- Input message: byte slice
- Symbol: fixed-size chunk of bytes (symbol_size)
- Source block: a group of K source symbols (K is per-block, after segmentation)
- Repair symbol: additional encoded symbol derived from a chosen neighbor set over intermediate symbols
- Intermediate symbols: source symbols plus precode-generated symbols (size N >= K)

We operate per source block.

## Determinism contract (non-negotiable)

Given:
- input bytes
- EncodingConfig / DecodingConfig
- explicit seed(s)

then:
- emitted symbol IDs and symbol bytes are deterministic
- decoder decisions (pivot selection, inactivation set, row operation order) are deterministic
- proof artifact bytes are deterministic

No ambient RNG, no wall-clock.

## Encoder outline (implementable contract)

1) Segmentation
- Split input bytes into fixed symbol_size chunks, pad deterministically (document padding rule).
- Partition into source blocks with at most K_max symbols each; document block sizing policy.

2) Precode / intermediate symbols
- Define a deterministic precode that maps K source symbols -> N intermediate symbols.
- Precode structure should be sparse, stable, and deterministic.
- Precode parameters are explicit in config (and included in proof artifact metadata).

3) Systematic emission
- Emit source symbols first in a deterministic order.

4) Repair symbol generation
For each repair symbol ID:
- deterministically choose a degree d via robust soliton distribution parameters (c, delta, etc.)
- deterministically select d neighbors among intermediate symbols via seeded RNG + stable sampling rule
- compute repair symbol as XOR (or GF(256) linear combination if needed) of neighbors

Neighbor selection and equation construction must be reproducible given (block_id, symbol_id, seed).

## Decoder outline (implementable contract)

We decode per block, maintaining a linear system over GF(256) (or XOR-only when valid).

1) Ingest
- track which symbols received and their IDs
- reject duplicates deterministically with a precise RejectReason

2) Peeling / belief propagation
- repeatedly solve degree-1 equations, substitute into others
- deterministic processing order for degree-1 queue

3) Inactivation decoding
When peeling stalls:
- choose an inactivation set deterministically (rule-based selection)
- perform deterministic elimination / pivoting (stable row ordering + stable pivot choice)

4) Completion
- recover intermediate symbols
- recover source symbols
- reassemble bytes and validate padding rule

## Proof-carrying decode trace artifact

For each decoded block, emit a compact artifact that allows offline verification / explainability:
- config + seeds + block sizing metadata
- which equations (symbol IDs + neighbor sets) were used
- the elimination trace (pivots, inactivation choices, row ops) in a bounded representation
- final outcome: success or RejectReason

The artifact is not required to be a full formal proof, but must be:
- deterministic
- bounded-size (explicit caps)
- sufficient to reproduce the decoder state transitions and explain failures

## Acceptance criteria

- Spec is detailed enough that an implementation can proceed without repeated external lookups.
- Every place we diverge from RFC 6330 is documented as an explicit design decision.
- Defines the precise determinism contract for symbol IDs, neighbor selection, and pivoting.
- Defines required proof-artifact contents and size bounds.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:12:45.176165815Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:39:14.335495249Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T02:39:14.327057948Z","close_reason":"Added RFC-6330-grade RaptorQ spec + determinism contract to docs/integration.md","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","decoding","encoding","raptorq"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1zho","depends_on_id":"bd-201j","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:22:51Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1zkki","title":"[SPORK][App][Core] AppSpec + AppHandle (start/stop/join, region-owned)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-06T05:43:30.188898874Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T05:44:15.769143719Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["app","otp","spork"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1zkki","depends_on_id":"bd-sd083","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:36:12Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":271,"issue_id":"bd-1zkki","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Scope

Define the **public** application-facing types:

- `AppSpec` (pure data / builder): describes topology and required capabilities.
- `AppHandle`: handle returned from start.
- `AppStartError` / `AppStopError`: explicit error taxonomy.

## Semantics (must be explicit, testable)

- `start(cx)`:
  - allocates (or is given) a root region
  - compiles `AppSpec` into supervisor/child specs
  - starts children deterministically
  - returns `AppHandle` that owns the root region

- `stop(cx)`:
  - requests cancel on root
  - drains + finalizes
  - returns only when region close implies quiescence

- `join(cx)`:
  - waits for terminal outcome of the root region (including crashes/cancel)

## Invariants

- close => quiescence (no live tasks, no pending obligations, finalizers empty)
- all stop paths are cancel-correct (request -> drain -> finalize)
- no ambient authority: an AppSpec cannot "secretly" reach globals

## Acceptance

- Minimal API compiles and can start a toy app under lab runtime.
- `AppHandle` drop semantics are explicit (either auto-stop or leak-preventing panic; pick and document).
- Doc tests show the recommended usage pattern.
","created_at":"2026-02-06T05:44:15Z"}]}

  - starts children deterministically
  - returns `AppHandle` that owns the root region

- `stop(cx)`:
  - requests cancel on root
  - drains + finalizes
  - returns only when region close implies quiescence

- `join(cx)`:
  - waits for terminal outcome of the root region (including crashes/cancel)

## Invariants

- close => quiescence (no live tasks, no pending obligations, finalizers empty)
- all stop paths are cancel-correct (request -> drain -> finalize)
- no ambient authority: an AppSpec cannot "secretly" reach globals

## Acceptance

- Minimal API compiles and can start a toy app under lab runtime.

- Doc tests show the recommended usage pattern.

 {"id":"bd-1zok9","title":"[SPORK] GenServer: call/cast/reply With Obligation Linearity","description":"GenServer epic: provide an OTP-grade `call`/`cast`/`reply` experience on top of existing asupersync primitives:

Core requirement: replies and other effects cannot be silently dropped under cancellation; all outcomes are explicit and deterministic.","design":"Design notes:

 {"id":"bd-2dbf","title":"HPACK: Huffman invalid padding silently accepted (FIXED)","description":"# Bug Fix: Huffman Invalid Padding (COMPLETED)

## Status: FIXED in session 2026-01-31

## Location
`src/http/h2/hpack.rs` - huffman_decode function

## Vulnerability
The Huffman decoder silently accepted invalid padding (not all 1s) at the end
of encoded data. Per RFC 7541, padding must be the most-significant bits of EOS.
Invalid padding could indicate encoding errors or malicious data.

## Original Code
```rust
// End of decoding - didn't check padding validity
Ok(String::from_utf8(output).map_err(|_| H2Error::compression("invalid UTF-8"))?)
```

## Fix Applied
Validate padding bits are all 1s:

```rust
// After decoding, check remaining bits are valid padding
if bit_offset > 0 {
    // Remaining bits should be all 1s (padding is MSBs of EOS symbol)
    let padding_mask = (1u8 << (8 - bit_offset)) - 1;
    let padding_bits = current_byte & padding_mask;
    if padding_bits != padding_mask {
        return Err(H2Error::compression("invalid Huffman padding"));
    }
}
```

## Testing Required
- test_huffman_decode_invalid_padding
- test_huffman_decode_valid_padding
- Fuzz test: Random byte sequences","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-01T02:57:13.121666105Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T02:57:31.914317531Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T02:57:31.914150501Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2dbf","depends_on_id":"bd-1bic","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:09Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2de4","title":"[ALIEN][PLAN] Rewrite rule set + side-condition checker","description":"## Purpose

Encode the lawful rewrites:
- semiring/monoid laws (when declared by policy)
- join/race/timeout algebraic rewrites
- cancellation/obligation laws (loser draining, finalize ordering)

## Deliverables

- Each rule is defined as:
  - pattern -> replacement
  - side conditions (checked)
  - explanation text (for galaxy-brain)

## Acceptance criteria

- Rules are deterministic and only fire when conditions hold.
- Unit tests cover each rule with a small witness plan.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:22.783688540Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T06:39:18.771355282Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T06:39:18.771199602Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","plan","rewrites"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2de4","depends_on_id":"bd-1cac","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:32Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2de4","depends_on_id":"bd-1cir","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:32Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2de4","depends_on_id":"bd-26v8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:32Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2dfoo","title":"[SPORK][Obs] Evidence Ledger Schema + Rendering","description":"Define Spork-level evidence ledgers for supervisor/registry/link decisions, plus a deterministic rendering format.

Alien-artifact goal: every decision can be explained with an evidence ledger entry.","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance:

 {"id":"bd-3dre","title":"[ALIEN][TRACE][GEO] Test suite: soundness + equivalence + cost regression","description":"## Purpose

Provide high-confidence correctness for normalization.

## Test categories

1) Soundness: output schedule is a linear extension of the poset.
2) Equivalence: reordering only swaps independent events (no dependency violations).
3) Determinism: same input trace => same output.
4) Optimality (bounded): for tiny traces, exact solver matches brute force.
5) Regression: known patterns reduce switch cost compared to Foata flatten.

## Acceptance criteria

- Property tests for (1)-(3).
- Exhaustive brute-force tests for n<=10 for (4).
- At least 3 targeted regression fixtures for (5).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:11:00.244269671Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T11:22:09.078430677Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T11:22:09.078361769Z","close_reason":"Test suite complete: 30 tests total (12 original + 8 Foata regression + 2 exhaustive brute-force + 1 determinism + 1 admissibility + 7 proptest property tests).  {"id":"bd-3f53","title":"NATS client: connect, publish, subscribe with Cx integration","description":"# NATS Client Implementation

## Overview
Pure Rust NATS client with full Cx integration, supporting publish/subscribe
and request/reply patterns with cancel-correct semantics.

## Background
NATS is a lightweight, high-performance messaging system. Key features:
- Subject-based routing (wildcards: *, >)
- At-most-once delivery (core)
- At-least-once delivery (JetStream)
- Request-reply pattern

## Design

### Connection Management
```rust
pub struct NatsClient {
    conn: TcpStream,  // Or TLS
    subscriptions: HashMap<u64, Subscription>,
    pending_replies: HashMap<String, oneshot::Sender<Message>>,
    next_sid: u64,
}

impl NatsClient {
    /// Connect to a NATS server.
    pub async fn connect(cx: &Cx, url: &str) -> Outcome<Self, NatsError> {
        // 1. Parse URL (nats://host:port)
        // 2. TCP connect
        // 3. Send CONNECT command with options
        // 4. Receive INFO from server
        // 5. Start background reader task in region
    }
    
    /// Connect with options (auth, TLS, etc.)
    pub async fn connect_with_options(
        cx: &Cx,
        urls: &[&str],  // Cluster URLs
        options: NatsOptions,
    ) -> Outcome<Self, NatsError>;
}
```

### Publish
```rust
impl NatsClient {
    /// Publish a message to a subject.
    pub async fn publish(
        &self,
        cx: &Cx,
        subject: &str,
        payload: &[u8],
    ) -> Outcome<(), NatsError> {
        cx.checkpoint()?;
        self.send_command(format!("PUB {} {}\r\n", subject, payload.len()))?;
        self.send_payload(payload)?;
        Ok(())
    }
    
    /// Publish with reply subject (for request pattern).
    pub async fn publish_request(
        &self,
        cx: &Cx,
        subject: &str,
        reply_to: &str,
        payload: &[u8],
    ) -> Outcome<(), NatsError>;
}
```

### Subscribe
```rust
impl NatsClient {
    /// Subscribe to a subject.
    pub async fn subscribe(
        &self,
        cx: &Cx,
        subject: &str,
    ) -> Outcome<Subscription, NatsError> {
        let sid = self.next_sid.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
        self.send_command(format!("SUB {} {}\r\n", subject, sid))?;
        
        let (tx, rx) = mpsc::channel(256);  // Bounded for backpressure
        self.subscriptions.lock().insert(sid, tx);
        
        Ok(Subscription { sid, rx, client: self.clone() })
    }
}

pub struct Subscription {
    sid: u64,
    rx: mpsc::Receiver<Message>,
    client: NatsClient,
}

impl Subscription {
    /// Receive next message. Cancellation-safe.
    pub async fn next(&mut self, cx: &Cx) -> Outcome<Option<Message>, NatsError> {
        cx.checkpoint()?;
        Ok(self.rx.recv().await)
    }
    
    /// Unsubscribe.
    pub async fn unsubscribe(self, cx: &Cx) -> Outcome<(), NatsError> {
        self.client.send_command(format!("UNSUB {}\r\n", self.sid))?;
        self.client.subscriptions.lock().remove(&self.sid);
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### Request/Reply
```rust
impl NatsClient {
    /// Request with automatic reply handling.
    pub async fn request(
        &self,
        cx: &Cx,
        subject: &str,
        payload: &[u8],
    ) -> Outcome<Message, NatsError> {
        // 1. Generate unique reply subject
        let reply_to = format!("_INBOX.{}", uuid::Uuid::new_v4());
        
        // 2. Subscribe to reply
        let (tx, rx) = oneshot::channel();
        self.pending_replies.lock().insert(reply_to.clone(), tx);
        
        // 3. Publish request
        self.publish_request(cx, subject, &reply_to, payload).await?;
        
        // 4. Wait for reply with timeout
        match cx.timeout(self.config.request_timeout, rx.recv()).await {
            Outcome::Ok(msg) => Ok(msg),
            Outcome::Cancelled => {
                self.pending_replies.lock().remove(&reply_to);
                Err(NatsError::Cancelled)
            }
            Outcome::Err(_) => Err(NatsError::Timeout),
        }
    }
}
```

## NATS Protocol Commands
- CONNECT: Client handshake
- INFO: Server info response
- PUB: Publish message
- SUB: Subscribe to subject
- UNSUB: Unsubscribe
- MSG: Message from server
- PING/PONG: Keepalive

## Cancellation Semantics
- Connection owned by region
- Region close sends UNSUB for all subscriptions
- Pending requests cancelled with cleanup
- Background reader task terminates

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Connect to NATS server
- [ ] Publish messages to subjects
- [ ] Subscribe with wildcard support
- [ ] Request/reply pattern works
- [ ] Backpressure on slow consumers
- [ ] Cancellation cleans up properly
- [ ] Unit tests for protocol parsing
- [ ] Integration tests with nats-server","notes":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- Protocol parsing (CONNECT, INFO, PUB, SUB, UNSUB, MSG, PING/PONG)
- Subject pattern matching (exact, *, > wildcards)
- Connection state machine transitions
- Request-reply inbox generation
- Backpressure queue behavior

### Integration Tests
- Connect to nats-server (Docker)
- Publish/subscribe round-trip
- Wildcard subscriptions
- Request-reply with timeout
- Multiple subscriptions
- Reconnection after disconnect

### Cancel-Correctness Tests
- Cancel during connect
- Cancel during publish (message delivery)
- Cancel during subscribe (unsubscribe sent)
- Cancel during request (cleanup pending)
- Region close with active subscriptions

### Lab Runtime Tests
- Deterministic message ordering
- Simulated latency
- Connection failure simulation
- Reproducible subscription timing

### Logging Requirements
```rust
tracing::info!(
    subject = %subject,
    payload_size = payload.len(),
    reply_to = ?reply_to,
    "NATS publish"
);

tracing::debug!(
    sid = sid,
    subject = %subject,
    queue_group = ?queue,
    "NATS subscribe"
);
```

### Test Scripts
```bash
# Start NATS server
docker run -d --name nats -p 4222:4222 nats:latest

# Run NATS tests
NATS_URL=nats://localhost:4222 cargo test nats::
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WindyStream","created_at":"2026-02-01T01:23:55.911814895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:26:16.198025981Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T21:26:16.197946012Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ecosystem-parity","messaging","nats"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3f53","depends_on_id":"bd-2aiq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:11Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":111,"issue_id":"bd-3f53","author":"WindyStream","text":"Initial NATS client implementation in src/messaging/nats.rs.
